title: Get ECG Log List
description:
This endpoint retrieves a list of the user’s Electrocardiogram (ECG) log entries before or after a given day.

Note: The ECG API is for research use or investigational use only, and is not intended for clinical or diagnostic purposes.

parameters.name: user-id
parameters.description: The encoded ID of the user. Use "-" (dash) for current logged-in user.

parameters.name: beforeDate
parameters.description: The date in the format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss. Only yyyy-MM-dd is required. Either beforeDate or afterDate must be specified.

parameters.name: afterDate
parameters.description: The date in the format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss. Only yyyy-MM-dd is required. Either beforeDate or afterDate must be specified.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: The sort order of entries by date. Use asc (ascending) when using afterDate. Use desc (descending) when using before date.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The number of entries returned. See Limit / Offset for more information. Maximum: 10

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: See Limit / Offset for more information. Supported: 0

response_fields.name: ecgReadings : startTime
response_fields.description: The date and time when the reading was started on the device.

response_fields.name: ecgReadings : averageHeartRate
response_fields.description: The average heart rate of the user during the reading.

response_fields.name: ecgReadings : resultClassification
response_fields.description: See Classification of ECG results for more information. Supported: Atrial Fibrillation | Normal Sinus Rhythm | Inconclusive | Inconclusive: High heart rate | Inconclusive: Low heart rate

response_fields.name: ecgReadings : waveformSamples
response_fields.description: An array of integers representing the ECG waveform.

response_fields.name: ecgReadings : samplingFrequencyHz
response_fields.description: The frequency in hertz at which the Fitbit ECG app sampled the voltage. Supported: 250

response_fields.name: ecgReadings : scalingFactor
response_fields.description: The scaling factor used to convert waveform samples to ECG voltages in mV (mV = waveformSamples / scalingFactor). Supported: 10922

response_fields.name: ecgReadings : numberOfWaveformSamples
response_fields.description: The total number of samples in the reading

response_fields.name: ecgReadings : leadNumber
response_fields.description: The ECG lead being used to take the reading. The compatible Fitbit wrist-worn devices contain a single-lead. Therefore, the leadNumber will always be 1. Supported: 1

response_fields.name: ecgReadings : featureVersion
response_fields.description: The version of the ECG app running on the device. The format of the compatible wrist-worn device components’ version [x.xx-x.xx-x.xx] represents app-service-web respectively.

response_fields.name: ecgReadings : deviceName
response_fields.description: Hardware name of the compatible wrist-worn device used to take the reading.

response_fields.name: ecgReadings : firmwareVersion
response_fields.description: Firmware running on the compatible wrist-worn device used to take the reading.

response_fields.name: pagination : afterDate | beforeDate
response_fields.description: The afterDate or beforeDate parameter of the request (whichever was specified).

response_fields.name: pagination : limit
response_fields.description: The limit parameter of the request. Maximum: 10

response_fields.name: pagination : next
response_fields.description: The URL of the request that will fetch the next page of results.

response_fields.name: pagination : offset
response_fields.description: The offset parameter of the request.

response_fields.name: pagination : previous
response_fields.description: The URL of the request that will fetch the previous page of results.

response_fields.name: pagination : sort
response_fields.description: The sort parameter of the request.
